sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: The Eleventh Striker
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Detective Conan: The Eleventh Striker works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Eleventh Striker clicked for you, Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and mystery, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Degeneration
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Eleventh Striker clicked for you, Resident Evil: Degeneration (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: The Eleventh Striker?
faq.answer: Try Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven, Case Closed: The Crimson Love Letter, Resident Evil: Degeneration—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: The Eleventh Striker while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: The Eleventh Striker?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: The Eleventh Striker (2012) is best described as Animation, Mystery, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: The Eleventh Striker part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
